disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Brief warning of vision 'greying out' and lightheadedness immediately before the collapse
trigger_question_examples: What was the very last thing you remember before you woke up on the floor?
Did you have any warning it was about to happen?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, everything went grey and I felt really lightheaded for a few seconds, and then I was on the floor.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Occurred after standing still for about 45 minutes in a warm kitchen
trigger_question_examples: What were you doing right before it happened?
Had you been standing for a while, or in a hot room?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'd been standing at the bench cooking for about 45 minutes and the kitchen was pretty warm.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Bystander witnessed pallor, loss of postural tone and a rapid full recovery within about 30 seconds
trigger_question_examples: Did anyone see it happen — what did they notice?
How long were you unconscious and how quickly did you recover?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My roommate saw it. She said I went really pale and just slid down, and I was only out about 30 seconds before I came round and was fine.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Struck the back of the head on the floor with brief loss of consciousness; small tender occipital lump, no laceration
trigger_question_examples: Did you hurt yourself when you fell?
Where did you hit your head?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I hit the back of my head on the floor. There's a small sore lump there now but the skin isn't broken.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No chest pain, palpitations or breathlessness before the collapse; no exertion at the time
trigger_question_examples: Did you feel your heart racing or pounding beforehand?
Any chest pain or shortness of breath before you passed out?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, no chest pain and my heart wasn't racing. I was just standing there cooking, not exercising or anything.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No tongue biting, no incontinence and no post-event confusion or drowsiness
trigger_question_examples: Did you bite your tongue or lose control of your bladder?
Were you confused or drowsy after you came round?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I didn't bite my tongue or wet myself, and I wasn't confused afterwards — I knew where I was straight away.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Poor oral intake that day; feels thirsty with a dry mouth
trigger_question_examples: Have you had much to eat or drink today?
Do you feel thirsty or dry?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it I don't think I drank much water today and my mouth feels really dry.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No previous episodes of loss of consciousness
trigger_question_examples: Has anything like this ever happened before?
Is this the first time you've passed out?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, this is the first time I've ever fainted.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No family history of sudden death, inherited heart disease or unexplained collapse
trigger_question_examples: Has anyone in your family died suddenly or young, or had a heart rhythm problem?
Any family history of heart conditions or unexplained collapses?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Not that I know of — no one in my family has died suddenly or had heart rhythm problems.
